signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Annual Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Annual Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% (up from 2.0% prior)** | YES | HIGH confidence[1][2][3][4]

South Korea's Statistics Korea released March 2026 CPI data on April 2, 2026, showing **annual inflation at 2.2%** (slightly varying reports of 2.16%-2.2% YoY), up from 2.0% in Jan-Feb, driven by oil prices (+9.9% YoY) but below some forecasts (2.3%-2.4%) due to government fuel cap
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.raw:

signals.headline: No Whale Bets or Mispriced Tips Confirmed Trending | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
No Whale Bets or Mispriced Tips Confirmed Trending | NO | HIGH confidence**  
Search data shows no explicit X posts on recent whale moves, smart money signals, or flagged mispriced markets beyond top volumes; Nikita Bier's X product head exit by June 30 trades low at 7%, but lacks buzz[1]. Trending lacks breaking confirmations like ceasefires or launches to pivot odds[1][2].
